text:
Once upon a time, in a small village surrounded by dense woods, there lived a boy named Oliver. He loved exploring the forests near his home, and his favorite adventure was discovering hidden lakes and hearing the songs of ancient birds.

One day, as Oliver was wandering deeper into the woods than ever before, he stumbled upon an old, wooden hut tucked away among the trees. The hut smelled of pine and dust, and the door creaked ominously as he pushed it open. Inside, there sat an old lady who appeared weary yet kind.

"Good day," she said gently, "I've lived here alone for as long as I can remember, and I've never seen a visitor."

Oliver was curious and a little brave. "I'm just looking for some solitude to think," he lied convincingly. "Tell me about this hut."

"Weaver," the lady said softly, her voice laden with history. "Our lives here were once woven in threads of magic. You see
